theme: E4 cycle 6 — the FIRST measure/iterate cycle of the E4 decadal (per the design-spec per-cycle plan, 155-158 are measure/iterate, 159 is the decadal close). Run the 5-item Network-Legibility rubric through its owning lenses against the now-complete network surfaces (/network bands 1-5, the rendering /vaults/graph 40-node/22-edge topology, the c154 vault<->graph round-trip), then tighten the highest-value gaps WITHOUT regressing the green suite. III 7-step.
register: Tokyo Night dark-first; honest-topology (the orphan nodes are NAMED as honest-sparse-by-design rather than hidden or faked; per-type legend counts make the typed taxonomy self-honest about which kinds are load-bearing); <=2 fighting colors (cyan --color-link focus glow/links + purple --color-primary, no new accent); motion unchanged (no new motion; the focus scrollIntoView is reduced-motion-safe from c154); writing-guidelines AVOID honored (no marketing adjectives added; the newcomer 'no adjective violations' finding confirmed the page voice); a11y both modes — and this cycle PROMOTED both-modes axe into gate-4 permanently (the carried c154 seed), which immediately caught a link-in-text-block miss the dark-only gate would have shipped.
operator_scope: TWO forks confirmed at the plan gate (AskUserQuestion): (1) lens assessment = MULTI-AGENT WORKFLOW (4 independent adversarial lens agents — Diagram-Reviewer / Node-Operator / IA / Newcomer — scoring the rubric, mirroring E1's RLP, NOT inline self-assessment); (2) scope = MEASURE + ITERATE (full 7-step: score -> implement top fixes -> re-measure, not measure-only). COMMIT-ONLY (the E4 surface bundles its deploy later, operator standing instruction).

iii_7step.measure: Machine baseline GREEN before touching anything: build 162pp/0err; 65/65 Playwright gates; axe 0 AA both modes on /network + /vaults/graph + /vaults/Harness.aDNA (ad-hoc probe); Lighthouse /network 98 / graph 90 / vault 99 (all = c154 baselines). Eyeballed the actual rendered surfaces (the c154 lesson — the graph shipped blank + was never looked at): confirmed the round-trip back-link resolves correctly ('RareHarness' = Harness.aDNA display_name; my thumbnail mis-read it as 'RareArchive') and band-1 NetworkDiagram renders (640x384, opacity 1). THEN ran a 4-agent lens Workflow (430k subagent tokens, 114s) scoring the 5-item Network-Legibility rubric independently (agents given only neutral facts + paths + screenshots, NOT primed with my findings).
iii_7step.orient: 4-lens rubric (owning-lens score is authoritative). node_clarity 4/5/4/4; edge_honesty 3.5(Diag)/5/5/5; local_shared 4.5/5/5/5; navigability 4/4/4/4; join_scent 4.5/3(Node-Op)/4/4. TWO items below the GREEN floor of 4, exactly at their owning lenses: edge_honesty 3.5 (Diagram-Reviewer: the ~19 edgeless nodes stack into a tall dead column beside the connected tangle — the HONEST sparse topology reads as a broken render) and join_scent 3 (Node-Operator: step-3 'Opt into federation' names WHAT you can federate but gives no HOW/WHERE — no command, file, or pointer, unlike step-1's real git clone). Cross-confirmed two pre-existing weaknesses via DOM probe: the ?focus highlight stroke computed to #333 not cyan (the !important rect-stroke rule loses to Mermaid's inline node styling), and a stale node identity (graph shows 'aDNANetwork.aDNA/Venus/Alpha Lattice', canonical is now 'Network.aDNA').
iii_7step.select: 3 logical targets — biased to the two GREEN-blockers + the owning-lens top_fixes, all small copy/CSS/markup, deferring the heavier structural items. (T1) edge-honesty + legibility: a one-line honest-sparseness note in the /vaults/graph lede naming the unlinked count ('19 vaults carry no cited relationship yet, so they sit unconnected here — honest topology, not missing data'; also drops the protests-too-much 'Nothing here is decorative'); per-type counts in BOTH legends (graph + /network band 3 — federation 10, companion 7, supersedes 3, umbrella 1, partner 1); graph-tip names the keyboard /vaults/ path (was 'select any node', a promise keyboard users can't keep). (T2) join-scent + node-clarity copy on /network: step-3 gets the transmission-unit sentence (a curated Home.aDNA registry slice, never contents) + 'reviewable = author+inspect before push' grounding + a 'See how federation works ->' pointer (a LINK, not an over-promised CLI command — honest-topology discipline); step-1 gets a prereq+time cue ('~5 min; assumes git + Claude Code CLI; see Get started'); the hero defines 'vault' inline ('each a self-governing folder of project knowledge') and unifies 'Home vault'->'Home.aDNA'; band-1 caption marked 'Six representative' + the hub-and-spoke subtitle scoped to the inline diagram (the full graph is NOT hub-and-spoke). (T3) navigability + gate infra: the ?focus node gets a drop-shadow GLOW on the node group (filter, which Mermaid never sets on the group, so it wins where the rect-stroke override lost) + 4px stroke; the carried both-modes axe seed promoted into gate-4 (dark+light loop, +8 tests).
iii_7step.implement: 3 site files + 1 gate file. site/src/pages/network.astro — hero lead vault-definition + Home.aDNA unify + commons->registry anchor; band-1 subtitle hub-and-spoke scoped to the diagram + caption 'Six representative... (See all 40 below)'; band-3 subtitle 'across five kinds of relationship' + per-type legend counts (.le-count, muted); band-4 step-1 .run-note prereq/time + step-3 transmission-unit/reviewable/pointer; new edgeTypeCounts reducer + .le-count/.run-note styles (.run-note a UNDERLINED for link-in-text-block). site/src/pages/vaults/graph.astro — edgeTypeCounts + unlinkedCount reducers; lede honest-sparseness note; legend per-type counts; graph-tip keyboard /vaults/ path (underlined); focus glow (:global g.graph-focus drop-shadow + 4px stroke). site/tests/gates/gate-4-a11y.spec.ts — both-modes loop (dark + light via localStorage theme seed), 8->16 tests. VaultRelationshipBlock untouched.
iii_7step.re_measure: build PASS 162pp/0err. FIRST gate run after edits = 71/73 — the new both-modes gate-4 CAUGHT a real regression: my step-1 'Get started' link sat mid-sentence in muted text with text-decoration:none -> axe link-in-text-block (BOTH modes). Fixed by underlining .run-note a + .graph-tip a (in-text links need a non-colour cue). Re-run: 73/73 GREEN. axe 0 AA both modes on all 3 surfaces. Lighthouse: /network 98/100/100/100 (LCP 2.1s, CLS 0 — identical baseline); /vaults/graph 93/100/100/100 (LCP 2.8s, TBT 90ms, CLS 0 — slightly BETTER than the 90 baseline, within noise; the focus drop-shadow filter added no measurable cost); vault detail unchanged (99). Focus glow verified: getComputedStyle(g.graph-focus).filter = 'drop-shadow(rgb(125,207,255) 0 0 7px) drop-shadow(rgb(125,207,255) 0 0 2px)' — the focused node now reads by shape, not a single cyan stroke lost in the colour field.
iii_7step.record: this JSON + cycle log in the session + mission/STATE/campaign cascade

post_fix_self_rescore.method: Rosetta self-re-score traceable to the specific findings addressed (NOT a second Workflow run — proportionate for a measure cycle's validate step). edge_honesty 3.5->4: the sparseness note + per-type counts close the Diagram-Reviewer's two cheap findings (caption-reframe option c + legend-census med); the DEEPER layout fix (cluster the orphans into a labelled subgraph, option a) is a generator change carried to c156, so 4 not 5. join_scent 3->4: step-3 transmission-unit + pointer + step-1 prereq close the Node-Operator's exact asks. navigability 4->4.5: focus glow now unmistakable + keyboard path named. node_clarity 4->4.5: vault defined inline + Home.aDNA unified. NO item < 4 -> the rubric clears the GREEN floor; a confirming lens re-run is a c156 option.

change_summary: site/src/pages/network.astro — hero lead now DEFINES 'vault' inline ('each a self-governing folder of project knowledge') + unifies 'Home vault'->'Home.aDNA vault' + anchors 'shared commons'->'the registry other nodes can pull from' (Newcomer top_fix + Node-Op/IA Home-naming nit); band-1 subtitle scopes the hub-and-spoke claim to the inline diagram ('drawn here as a simple hub') + caption 'Six representative aDNA vaults... (See all 40 in the full graph below)' (Diagram-Rev/Newcomer — the full graph is NOT hub-and-spoke); band-3 'across five kinds of relationship' + per-type legend counts; band-4 step-1 prereq/time .run-note + step-3 transmission-unit ('a curated slice of your Home.aDNA registry... never their contents') + 'reviewable=author+inspect before push' + 'See how federation works ->' pointer (Node-Op top_fix, a LINK not an over-promised command). New edgeTypeCounts reducer + .le-count/.run-note styles; .run-note a UNDERLINED (link-in-text-block).

site/src/pages/vaults/graph.astro — lede honest-sparseness note naming unlinkedCount ('19 vaults carry no cited relationship yet, so they sit unconnected here — honest topology, not missing data') replacing the protests-too-much 'Nothing here is decorative' (Diagram-Rev top_fix, the cheap caption variant; the orphan-cluster LAYOUT fix is carried to c156); per-type legend counts; graph-tip names the keyboard '/vaults/ index' path + drops the internal 'relationship block' term (IA + Newcomer); ?focus node glow — :global(.mermaid-container g.graph-focus) drop-shadow(cyan) + 4px stroke (IA top_fix; the prior 3px !important rect-stroke computed to #333, losing to Mermaid's inline node styling — the group filter wins). edgeTypeCounts + unlinkedCount reducers; .le-count + .graph-tip a (underlined) styles.

site/tests/gates/gate-4-a11y.spec.ts — PROMOTED the carried c154 both-modes-axe seed: the 8 audited pages now run in dark AND light (light via localStorage.theme seed before load), 8->16 tests (total gates 65->73). On first run it immediately caught a link-in-text-block regression my own c155 edit introduced — proving the seed's value (the dark-only gate would have shipped it).

doctrine_compliance.honest_topology: STRENGTHENED — the cycle's spine. The ~19 edgeless nodes are now NAMED as honest-sparse-by-design (not hidden, not faked); the per-type legend counts make the typed taxonomy self-honest (federation/companion load-bearing; umbrella/partner rare); the step-3 copy names exactly what transmits and what does not ('never their contents'); ZERO fabricated edges. The deeper orphan-layout legibility fix is carried, not faked.
doctrine_compliance.two_fighting_colors: PASS — cyan --color-link (focus glow + links + legend counts stay muted) + purple --color-primary unchanged; no new accent; the focus glow reuses the link cyan.
doctrine_compliance.motion_budget: PASS — no new motion added; the only animated affordance is the c154 reduced-motion-safe focus scrollIntoView.
doctrine_compliance.no_marketing_adjectives: PASS — Newcomer lens explicitly found 'no marketing-adjective violations... admirably plain'; the new copy ('honest topology, not missing data', 'never their contents', 'Takes about five minutes') stays plain.
doctrine_compliance.dual_audience: PASS — newcomer gets 'vault' defined inline + the keyboard path named; the developer/operator gets the transmission unit, the prereqs, and the per-type census; the senior-engineer Diagram-Reviewer gets the sparseness named so the graph stops reading as a broken render.
doctrine_compliance.engine_files_unedited: PASS — skill_iii_cycle / skill_site_design_pipeline / skill_decadal_aar / the runners / the ADR-023 generator (build_vaults_data.mjs) + vaults.json + the .mmd all UNTOUCHED (the staleness + orphan-cluster fixes that WOULD touch the generator are deliberately carried). Only 3 site files + 1 gate spec changed.
doctrine_compliance.dark_first: PASS — verified both modes; the both-modes axe is now a permanent gate.
doctrine_compliance.a11y_mechanism: PASS — in-text links underlined (link-in-text-block, both modes); the focus glow is decorative on the aria-hidden svg; node nav stays pointer-only via non-focusable <g> (keyboard path = /vaults/ index, now NAMED on the page).

validation.build: PASS — astro build 162 pages, 0 errors; no route change; generator/data untouched (no prebuild churn).
validation.playwright_gates: 73/73 PASS (was 65/65; +8 from the both-modes gate-4 loop). First post-edit run was 71/73 — the new light+dark gate-4 caught a self-introduced link-in-text-block on /network (the step-1 'Get started' link, colour-only); fixed via underline -> 73/73.
validation.axe_wcag_aa: 0 violations BOTH modes on /network, /vaults/graph, /vaults/Harness.aDNA — now via the permanent gate-4 dark+light loop AND an ad-hoc confirming probe.

lighthouse.network: Perf 98 / A11y 100 / BP 100 / SEO 100 — LCP 2.1s, CLS 0 (identical to baseline; copy-only change)
lighthouse.graph: Perf 93 / A11y 100 / BP 100 / SEO 100 — LCP 2.8s, TBT 90ms, CLS 0 (>= the 90 baseline; the focus drop-shadow filter + legend counts added no measurable cost)
lighthouse.vault_detail: Perf 99 / A11y 100 — unchanged (VaultRelationshipBlock not edited this cycle)

validation.functional: PASS — round-trip back-link resolves correctly ('RareHarness' display_name, href /vaults/Harness.aDNA/, 40/40 nodes wired); focus glow computed filter = cyan drop-shadow (verified via getComputedStyle); per-type counts render (umbrella 1 / federation 10 / partner 1 / companion 7 / supersedes 3); sparseness note renders the live unlinkedCount.

network_legibility_self_check.1_node_clarity: STRENGTHENED (lens 4.25 -> ~4.5) — 'vault' now defined on first use in the hero; 'Home vault'/'Home.aDNA' unified; band-1 marked as a 6-of-40 representative sample.
network_legibility_self_check.2_edge_honesty: LIFTED off the floor (3.5 -> ~4) — the orphan column is named as honest-sparse-by-design + the legend carries per-type counts. Remaining (carried c156): the LAYOUT (cluster the 19 orphans into a labelled subgraph) and the supersedes-vs-companion dashed-line symbol collision + the aDNANetwork->Network.aDNA staleness regen (all generator/data-layer changes).
network_legibility_self_check.3_local_shared_boundary: HELD/STRENGTHENED (~5) — step-3 now names the transmission unit (a curated Home.aDNA registry slice, never contents) and to whom, closing the Node-Operator 'what flows on the wire' gap.
network_legibility_self_check.4_navigability: STRENGTHENED (lens 4.0 -> ~4.5) — the ?focus node is now unmistakable (cyan glow, not a lost 3px stroke) + the keyboard /vaults/ path is named on the graph. Carried: a keyboard-reachable node-twin list (the graph stays mouse-only for node->vault).
network_legibility_self_check.5_join_scent: LIFTED off the floor (3 -> ~4) — step-3 'Opt into federation' now has a concrete pointer + names what transmits; step-1 has the prereq + ~5-min time cue. Carried: a real publish command/affordance once the flow is verifiably shippable for a fresh node.

deferred_to_156plus: edge_honesty LAYOUT (the deeper half of the Diagram-Reviewer top finding): cluster the ~19 edgeless nodes into a labelled subgraph ('No cited relationship yet — 19') or a chip row, so the disconnected set reads as an intentional holding area, not a tall dead column. Generator change (touches the .mmd emission in build_vaults_data.mjs) — c156.

Stale node identity: the graph still renders 'aDNANetwork.aDNA / Venus / Alpha Lattice'; canonical is 'Network.aDNA' (renamed 2026-05-31). A vaults.json/.mmd regen from current Home.aDNA inventory — data-layer, pairs with the Home.aDNA edge-overlay upstream — c156 or the decadal.

supersedes-vs-companion symbol collision: both render as dashed edges; the 's' marker is sub-pixel at the graph's zoom. Give supersedes a genuinely distinct stroke OR annotate the legend. Generator/.mmd — c156.

Keyboard-reachable node-twin: a visually-hidden list of node->vault anchors parallel to the aria-hidden svg, so the graph round-trip is not mouse-exclusive (Diagram-Rev + IA + Node-Op + Newcomer all noted it; deliberate axe-clean tradeoff for now).
/vaults/graph LCP/Perf (now 93, was 90) — the 40-node client-side Mermaid layout; candidate pre-render-at-build or lazy/in-view render — c159 decadal.
Confirming lens re-run on the post-fix surfaces (a second 4-agent Workflow) — optional rigor for c156 if the operator wants the lifts independently verified.
E4 decadal close (AAR + full Lighthouse + STATE/STR cascade; ADR-033 — the network_edges.yaml overlay/adaptation seam — ratifies at the phase-exit gate per campaign SO #14) — cycle 159.
deploy: NOT deployed — commit-only (operator standing instruction; the whole E4 surface bundles its deploy later). Carry-forward for that deploy: the shared MermaidDiagram render fix from c154 means 12+ already-deployed concept/docs diagrams will start rendering — eyeball them on the next ship. #needs-human: rotate SS_VERCEL_TOKEN (recurring CLI-stdout leak class; carry-forward).

aar_lightweight.worked: Running the lenses as an independent multi-agent Workflow (operator's fork) surfaced TWO below-floor items (edge_honesty 3.5, join_scent 3) that a self-assessment would likely have rated 4+ — the independence earned its keep. Eyeballing the actual rendered surfaces (the c154 lesson) was essential: the graph's orphan-column problem is invisible in source and in element-presence gates.
aar_lightweight.didnt: A measure cycle still introduced a regression (the colour-only step-1 link) — caught only because this same cycle promoted the both-modes axe gate. Self-review of my own copy edits for link-in-text-block would have caught it pre-build.
aar_lightweight.finding: The carried both-modes-axe seed paid off on its FIRST run by catching a same-cycle regression — strong evidence for making it permanent (done). And the deepest legibility problems are STRUCTURAL/DATA (orphan layout, stale names), not copy — they need a generator/data cycle (c156), which is why a measure cycle could only lift edge_honesty to the floor, not to 5.
aar_lightweight.change: Underline in-text links by default in new components (don't set text-decoration:none on a link embedded in body text). Keep the both-modes axe gate.
aar_lightweight.follow_up: c156 should be a DATA/generator cycle: cluster the orphan nodes (edge_honesty to 5) + regen for the Network.aDNA name + the supersedes stroke. Optionally re-run the lens Workflow to confirm the c155 lifts.

notes: First E4 measure/iterate cycle, and the first time the network surfaces were scored against the Network-Legibility rubric by independent lenses. The headline: the rubric WORKED — it found exactly two below-floor items at their owning lenses (edge_honesty, join_scent), both now lifted off the floor with cheap, honest copy/CSS. The honest-topology discipline cut both ways this cycle: it is WHY the graph looks sparse (no fabricated edges) and the reason the fix had to NAME the sparseness rather than hide it. The both-modes axe gate (carried from c154) justified itself immediately by catching a same-cycle link-in-text-block regression. The deeper legibility wins (orphan clustering, name staleness, distinct supersedes stroke) are data/generator work deliberately carried to c156, keeping c155 a clean, reviewable copy+CSS+gate cycle with the generator/data layer untouched.
